mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off repricing, depressing equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE jumps >0.5pp, or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × HOUST decline) fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 300, aggressive federal funds rate hikes force equity risk premiums higher, causing sustained negative forward returns as valuation multiples compress.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPI exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the predictive power of the CPI-FEDFUNDS spread vanishes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income and consumer credit expansion fuel aggregate demand growth, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive relationship with concurrent rises in RSAFS and GDPC1, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5% signals supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints that increase input costs and compress margins, driving negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns do not decline following INDPRO drops >1.5%, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates a labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, triggering a negative earnings revision cycle and depressing S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint movement of UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market overheating where housing starts exceed 1500 units, rising mortgage rates subsequently crowd out consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when HOUST exceeds 1500 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the predictive relationship breaks down in post-2008 data samples.
